title: CC2 Wake Voltage Valid Range
requirement: When CC2 voltage is 4.8V to 7.7V, the VCU shall accept it as a valid wake-up signal and output vehicle_state=170, vehicle_mode=5, ready_flag=1.

title: CC2 Voltage Below Valid Range
requirement: When CC2 voltage is below 4.8V, the VCU shall output test_status=4, vehicle_state=30, vehicle_mode=2, ready_flag=0.

title: CC2 Voltage Above Valid Range
requirement: When CC2 voltage exceeds 7.8V and is not the sleep trigger 12.0V, the VCU shall output test_status=4, vehicle_state=30, vehicle_mode=2, ready_flag=0.

title: Sleep Trigger Voltage
requirement: When CC2 voltage equals 12.0V with sleep command type=2, the VCU shall output test_status=3, vehicle_state=30, vehicle_mode=2, ready_flag=0.

title: CC Voltage Cable Check
requirement: When CC voltage is 0.1V to 3.9V, the VCU shall output test_status=4 and vehicle_state=30 because the cable contact resistance is invalid.

title: CP Amplitude Interference Check
requirement: When CP amplitude is 9.1V to 12.9V, the VCU shall output test_status=4 and vehicle_state=30 because the CP signal conflicts with the charging protocol.

title: Supply Voltage Overvoltage Check
requirement: When supply voltage is 9.1V to 15.9V, the VCU shall output test_status=4 and vehicle_state=30 because unexpected external supply or overvoltage is detected.

title: Network Wake Conflict Check
requirement: When network wake enable state equals 1, the VCU shall output test_status=4 and vehicle_state=30 because network wake conflicts with CC2 wake protocol.

title: READY Flag Consistency
requirement: When vehicle_state is 170, the VCU shall set ready_flag=1. When vehicle_state is 30, the VCU shall set ready_flag=0.

title: Test Duration Compliance
requirement: When a single-signal VCU test is executed, the actual_duration shall be below 120 seconds.
